etymology.description: 尸(尸) (shī, "corpse") + 匕(匕) (bǐ, "ladle") = man sitting or leaning on another man -> to stop or close; intimate
etymology.interpretation: The character 尼 (ní, "nun") is composed of 尸 (shī, "corpse"), depicting a person, and 匕 (bǐ, "ladle"), which originally conveyed the idea of one person leaning on or closely associated with another, leading to the sense of being intimate or stopping. The association with nuns likely derives from the original sense of close association.

examples.chinese: 这位尼姑总是在清晨祈祷(這位尼姑總是在清晨祈禱)。
examples.pinyin: Zhè wèi nígū zǒng shì zài qīngchén qídǎo.
examples.english: The nun always prays in the early morning.

examples.chinese: 小镇上的尼庵非常宁静(小鎮上的尼庵非常寧靜)。
examples.pinyin: Xiǎo zhèn shàng de ní'ān fēicháng níngjìng.
examples.english: The nunnery in the small town is very peaceful.
